description: Sources and candidate corpora for disappeared-person records and portraits. Each person record in data/persons/*.json carries a 'field_sources' map whose values are the 'id' fields below, recording which source is authoritative for each selected field (including 'portrait'). Candidate-only sources may appear in portrait_candidates but must not become field_sources until reviewed and selected.

sources.name: Sitios de Memoria Uruguay
sources.licence: CC BY-SA 4.0 (verify per item before public release)
sources.notes: Primary structured metadata source (open dataset + per-person pages). Portraits only when present in the 'field-fotografia' container; person pages also carry work posters and materials that must not be used as portraits.

sources.name: Madres y Familiares de Uruguayos Detenidos Desaparecidos
sources.licence: Verify reuse terms before public release
sources.notes: Foundational Uruguayan organisation (since 1983). Published photo + personal data per disappeared person from the mid-1980s; maintains a galeria de imagenes. Strong authoritative portrait source for Uruguayan victims.

sources.name: Parque de la Memoria (Argentina)
sources.licence: Verify reuse terms before public release
sources.notes: Argentine state memorial database. Best portrait source for victims detained/disappeared in or linked to Argentina (e.g. Cóndor cases).

sources.name: Investigación Histórica sobre Detenidos Desaparecidos (Presidencia)
sources.licence: Public official report; verify reuse before public release
sources.notes: Official government investigation with per-person fichas (photo, personal data, detention and disappearance data). The bound tomos are mirrored locally under doc/.

sources.name: Local tracked fotos-desaparecidos portrait corpus
sources.notes: Tracked local portrait files supplied with the project. Use as review-only candidate imagery; do not treat filename matches as authoritative identity or provenance evidence.
